title: The Adventure of the Creeping Man
synopsis: Professor Presbury, a distinguished biologist at a university, has been behaving strangely since becoming engaged to a woman half his age — prowling at night, climbing the ivy on his house, and attacking his own dog. His assistant Bennett brings the case to Holmes, who traces a mysterious correspondence from Prague.
solution: Professor Presbury has been injecting himself with a serum derived from langur monkeys — obtained from a Prague scientist named Lowenstein — in an attempt to restore his youth and vitality for his young fiancee; the injections are causing him to temporarily revert to ape-like behaviour.
notable_elements: One of the most science-fiction-adjacent stories in the Canon, anticipating themes of body modification
Holmes links the case to his monograph on the uses of dogs — his dog Roy's behaviour was the first clue
The story is set shortly before Holmes's retirement from practice
Raises ethical questions about the limits of science in the pursuit of youth and love
